I cannot recommend Rematch as of right now.

As someone who got very far into competitive Rocket League, (Top 3% of players at my peak and ~3,000 hours, truly no life) I was incredibly excited for Rematch. This is because Rocket League got killed by Epic Games and hasn't really been interesting to me and a large portion of players in years.

Rematch was referred to by many (including myself) as the Rocket League killer. This is because the potential of a game like Rematch is limitless. Fifa on the other end of the spectrum for soccer games has always been incredibly lazy with their design, so getting a hybrid between something like Rocket League a sandbox sports game with it's own incredibly well designed unique physics and Fifa a realistic soccer game, was incredible. I bought into every moment of it's uprise.

I participated in every Beta playtest. I played the game almost daily for about the first 2-3 months of its release. There are several problems with the game that have led me to stop playing. I admittedly still check in with the game every month or so to see if it's playable, and as of this review it is not.

The first major and most obvious problem with this game are the bugs. There are bugs intertwined with every part of this game, so much so that it feels insulting that you've payed any amount of money to play. This ranges from completely broken gameplay, to queing into ranked games with not enough players (without the match being cancelled), and even to character/clothing models clashing in character customization.

The fact that I have payed any amount of money to play a game this broken is incredibly insulting. It unironically feels like the Devs have used ChatGPT to code this game. If you follow the Patch Notes, even the most recent Patch being Patch #6 is riddled with things that should've been fixed before release. We are 6 MONTHS into it's release. The Devs are making new game modes (that are equally as broken) instead of fixing the issues that they already know exist. 

My second main problem with this game is the balancing issues. The coolest and most unique part of Rocket League is that gameplay and physics wise, everything is IDENTICAL to the release of the game, other than the hitboxes of the cars themselves. Fundamental game mechanics such as flipping, hitting the ball, and how fast the game moves have never changed. THAT is why the skill ceiling for Rocket League is higher than any other game out there. Players have been allowed to practice mechanics in a stable environment for a decade at this point.

So when every single Patch in Rematch changes the core gameplay loop and how it feels, players notice. There's no reward for learning mechanics and strategies because the balance of the game is STILL BEING DEVELOPED. On this point, I can be a little understanding. Balancing a game like this which is brand new to the scene from a company without a lot of resources is admittedly difficult. The main issue is that these decisions are being made AFTER it's release.

I think majority of my issues with this game would be solved if the Devs would admit that it's not finished and stop charging people to even play it.

If this game were free to play, it would allow WAY more players in, therefore way more testing capabilities for balancing. If players WANT to support the game, they can participate in micro transactions like literally every other game offers nowadays, INCLUDING this one. If the players just want to try your broken game (that is so far from finished it hurts) they can just try it, help you guys balance the game more, and everyone wins.

The part that hurts so much is that when the game decides to not be buggy (maybe one out of every ten games) it actually is incredibly fun. I really do see the potential for this game so it hurts to write a review like this.

TL:DR - This game is too broken gameplay and balancing wise for you to pay anything for it right now imo. That might change in the future if the devs ever figure out how to fix the bugs and balance the game though. It has a LOT of potential in my eyes.

This game was fun at launch with friends, but the game has too many bugs and exploits to really enjoy as a casual player. Not to mention, the game frequently faces server problems unlike any other. I remember playing the beta and averaging 35 ping like any normal competitive multiplayer game. Since release, I have never gone below 75 ping. 

This game's community is also the worst. It is the same problem that faces Rocket League, but way worse because the casual players have all left this game. The community/game is stuck with all of these people that don't pass, don't play goalie, and ragequit from totally winnable setbacks. I've had games where the enemy team scores a one-off, fluke goal in the first 30 seconds and both of my teammates will leave, despite there still being 5 and a half minutes left in the match. Nobody that plays game really believes in the art and joy of a comeback and it is very boring.

It is very sad, because I remember playing the beta with all of my friends and playing at launch and everyone had a good time and were very friendly. Nobody faced the previous problems I talked about either, but all the casual players left as these problems arose. Now the only people that really play the game are these "BlueLock" wannabes that use every exploit that the devs refuse to fix, like the "Ippy slide" which is a bug/game-breaking exploit that has been in the game since launch this past summer.

Definitely has been my favorite competitive game of this year. I will continue to play for a long while for sure. I think unfortunately the developers are either too small of a team or do not prioritize the correct things to solve the issues that the game has. I don't think the game has super long-term prospects since the game lacks different modes of play. It is just Soccer, which is definitely a super solid idea for a while, but eventually I think most players will want more and the game simply does not offer that.

I really think forcing random players to play goalkeeper brings down the experience generally for many.
- It forces players to play a role they may not want to fill
- This leads to very unskilled keepers which can bring down the experience for the rest of the team
- It is also the hardest role and usually the least involved as well which is a bad combo

As a solution I think the game should've leaned harder on it's arcade feeling and made the game NOT to be a soccer-sim (without truly simulating) and could've been more of an arcade soccer experience. We could've had a number of ways to score goals that do not involve a dedicated keeper. Something similar to how they are handling their 1v1 gate-opening mechanic or even the fireball gamemode scoring system are good examples that makes everyone feel equally a part of the pitch play.

Overall though, I think the game is super fun when it works and has produced some of the most fun I have had with friends and even random people as well. It has a low skill floor and a relatively medium skill ceiling compared to other competitive games. A lot of what really pushes the games difficulty is getting your team to work together which feels amazing when you have a full stack with comms but feels not-so-good otherwise. I know the game will go free-to-play one day but I hope when it does, they solve most if not all of the current issues that keep players from coming back.

reviews.review:
Sur le papier, REMATCH propose un concept vraiment novateur, et il le fait plutôt bien… en théorie.
Mais après sept mois de sortie, le constat reste le même : le jeu est toujours rempli de bugs. Certes, il y en a moins qu’au lancement, mais ceux qui persistent sont loin d’être anecdotiques, au contraire, ce sont souvent les plus gênants.

Ils avaient présenté un nouveau mode de jeu dans une vidéo, et il a fallu attendre un mois pour enfin le voir arriver. Résultat : il est resté moins d’une semaine, et au passage ils ont désactivé les autres modes de jeu… pour un mode qui, de toute façon, était injouable en soloQ, car tout le monde veut absolument être le main character.

Pour un jeu multijoueur, il y a bien trop de fonctionnalités manquantes ou arrivées trop tard, sans la moindre explication sur leur utilité. Un exemple parfait : le système de signalement. Il existe, oui. Mais on ne sait pas à quoi il sert, si quelque chose est réellement fait, ni comment sont gérées les sanctions. C’est le néant total.

Et parlons-en des sanctions : quitter une partie, ce que tout le monde fait quand il n’est pas content, y compris en ranked, déclenche à chaque fois un message d’avertissement du style :

“Attention, vous pourriez recevoir une pénalité.”
Sauf qu’on n’a aucune idée de ce que sont ces pénalités, ni si elles existent réellement. En même temps, vu le peu de joueurs restants, s’ils commencent à bannir les gens sur le court terme, il ne restera plus grand monde…

Le jeu peut être vraiment fun, mais uniquement s’il n’y a pas de bugs et si vous y jouez avec des amis. Parce qu’en l’état, il n’y a presque plus personne sur Steam, ce qui oblige souvent à tomber sur des joueurs consoles, fréquemment toxiques au possible.

Et pour couronner le tout, les seules nouveautés régulières qu’on voit débarquer sont… des packs de skins Balenciaga, Adidas et compagnie. Rien de vraiment utile, rien qui améliore l’expérience de jeu.

Bref : un jeu avec du potentiel, sympa quand tout fonctionne, mais plombé par des bugs persistants, un manque de clarté, des fonctionnalités absentes ou mal gérées, et un contenu additionnel qui semble complètement déconnecté des vrais problèmes et une communauté des plus discutables.

Играл в Rematch несколько часов и, к сожалению, остался разочарован. Идея у игры неплохая, но реализация подводит. Управление кажется неотзывчивым, а бои ― повторяющимися и быстро надоедающими. Оптимизация тоже оставляет желать лучшего: просадки FPS случаются даже в самых простых сценах.

Сюжет вроде бы есть, но подан настолько рвано, что к персонажам привязаться невозможно. Визуально игра выглядит устаревшей, а интерфейс перегружен и неудобен.

Пока что проект ощущается сырым. Возможно, со временем его доведут до ума, но в нынешнем состоянии я не вижу причин рекомендовать Rematch.

UNE FRAPPE EN PLEINE LUCARNE ! (Mon avis après 500h de jeu)
Écoutez j'ai plus de 500 heures au compteur sur REMATCH, donc je peux vous garantir que ce jeu est une tuerie si vous aimez le vrai esprit d'équipe.

Ce n'est pas une simulation photo-réaliste, loin de là. Mais dans la façon de jouer ensemble, de se placer sur le terrain et de vraiment collaborer, je trouve ça MILLE FOIS plus réaliste que ces jeux qui se disent "simulations" mais où tout le monde joue perso. C'est ça le vrai foot.

Les + :
C'est le kiff total si t'aimes le jeu d'équipe. La sensation de foot est super bien rendue.

Le prix est super correct pour le nombre d'heures que tu peux y mettre.

Tu as une vraie sensation de faire partie d'un collectif.

Les - :
Oui, y a des petits bugs qui traînent parfois, mais ça reste largement gérable.

⚠️ Le point noir : Comme dans tous les jeux d'équipe en ligne, si tu tombes sur des joueurs solistes qui font n'importe quoi, qui se croient tout seuls sur le terrain, tu vas galérer et ce n'est pas fun. Il faut avoir de la chance avec son équipe ou jouer avec des potes motivés, mais ça, c'est la vie des jeux coop, on s'y fait !

Mon verdict :

Franchement, si t'es prêt à t'investir un peu dans le collectif, c'est un super bon jeu de foot.

this game is...how do i put this...horrible. its plagued with net code issue, priority issues, and everything else you could muster up. your buddy will lob a ball to you and you will have a head pass queued up to pass it into the goal or wherever. you'll jump up to headbutt it and it will go straight through your head to the guy standing behind you on the ground and he headbutts it away. every patch or update they release fails to fix any of these issues and in fact makes them worse. this game legit functioned better in the beta than it does now. lord knows i understand that these are issues that can occur but to see no progress made towards fixing these issues(and them making it worse) while they are releasing new content like limited time game modes and such shows that the development of the game doesn't have its priorities straight. And this game has fully released. Im curious at this point if they are even truly testing their updates or just releasing patches and seeing what happens. But this kind of stuff shouldnt be standard with the full release stable branch of the game. That's literally why test branches exist in games. its for people to test out under real world conditions so the company can receive feedback on whether a patch has fixed issues or not.  LONG STORY SHORT, game played better in beta, dev team focusing on new game modes, and updates dont address/fix current game breaking issues. DO NOT BUY. WASTE OF $30 for a FULL RELEASE BROKEN UNPOLISHED GAME

It’s like everyone is suffering from main-character syndrome, either they’re hogging every goal attempt like they’re the chosen one, or they Alt+F4 the second the game dares to inconvenience them. And if they’re even one goal behind? Forget it, they leave immediately like the game personally offended them. Teamplay might as well be a mythical concept for most of these players, and the sheer level of learning resistance on display is astonishing. They’ll repeat the same idiotic move five times and act genuinely shocked when it fails the sixth. I’ve seen goldfish with better situational awareness.

And don’t get me started on the gameplay mechanics. They’re luck-based to a laughable degree. If I don’t catch the ball with pixel-perfect precision, tough luck, I lose it instantly. Meanwhile, my opponent just has to exist somewhere vaguely near the ball, and it automatically curves into their hands like the game owes them a favor. It’s less a test of skill and more a cosmic joke where the dice are perpetually loaded against you.

Do I recommend buying this? Yes, ONLY if it's discounted, though if you want, you can purchase the game at a full price. I've started playing this game since beta but recently purchased it on season 1. My friend had this beta access so he gave me some as well. The gameplay is really unique, I mean like in a way that it has fresh mechanics that for me seems balanced and fair for some. They have different types of game modes which is 3v3, 4v4, and 5v5. Although there are good things, there can also be bad. So far, the only bad experience I have encountered is the optimization. 

Really, the optimization is the only thing frustrating about this game, well you could also include the matchmaking but I think that would be reasonable for it to be not being free. Imagine the ball being passed to you, then you go near to the area which you now hold the ball. When you try to kick it, the kick animation will occur, suddenly someone steals it, and now you have to wait for the animation to end before you get to control your character again. And when that happens, the player already is far from you which really bugs me so much. 

Some of the reviews base on the community from different social media, they say that they lack game mechanics which makes the game really boring. Honestly I will have to disagree with that. There are tons of mechanics and to me, I would really say they are quite alright. Maybe in the next season they would update it, hopefully. Another thing is the battlepass which here I will have to agree. Basically you earn this battlepass coins by playing, but you have to purchase it to unlock exclusive items. Though the battlepass will also return the exact cost you spent, I still think a battlepass system should not exist in this game, because you literally already spent money to buy it. Then again, it is modern gaming.

Though the updates are continuing and some adjustments, also new aesthetics which I will say is really amazing, for some, the only issue I can really find is the optimization. It's fun to play with friends, solo not so much, unless you're an egoist especially that love blue lock and some soccer, you'll find way to love it.

First of all, the idea behind this game is remarkable and at the same time, way too belated due to demand, passion through football all over the world. It may because of EA sports which supresses other franchises and become a monopoly on this kind of football theme games.

I loved the game, i loved the controls, i played the beta version which was very good also. I pre-purchased rematch and even sent emails to developers whether the game may be dropped earlier. For my freetime after the work, the best option is playing rematch at the moment. But here is the catch (which is very understandable) the game has a horrible live community. I am NOT talking about you who is reading this, the bad community of this game; during the game they send emotes every second, they hinder the game by randomly shoot the ball to their goal when they dont get passed everytime they want the ball. After 2-0 generally such players create a chaos, dont care if they win or lose or have some beautiful time, they want the mess and end up blocking the game obviously. After that point we stay as 4 players on our side and 6 on competitors. After that when some player leaves, there will be no consequences afterwards. Which means anyone can leave any moment whenever they like.

I believe, the reason behind this, is; the game is brand new, just came out this summer which means new players are needed, the amount of players rn is not enough to convince developers to add policy for such players. Overall, i really the enjoyed playing the game so far, i love what they are doing with this game, and for future as a player i suggest to add a policy for trolls, and lower the system requirments in order to reach higher amount of live players.

На юбилейных ста часах пора бы и отзыв написать.

Что действительно заслуживает похвалы - кор геймплей. Сделано хорошо, и самое главное - свежо. На фоне тысячной фифы в особенности. И в это хочется играть. И на этом плюсы игры, по большому счету, заканчиваются, как и "хочется играть" себя понемногу  изживает ко второй сотне часов.

Разработчики за эти полгода существования игры не добавили НИЧЕГО, что заставляло бы возвращаться в игру. И речь даже не о каких-то отдельных режимах игры вроде (о боже упаси) файрболла. Вариантов, казалось бы, масса, но разработчики, кажется, уже давно отдыхают где-нибудь с девочками на Пхукете. Хватило бы банального - возможности игроков объединяться в кланы/группы со своими лидербордами, другими словами - реально соревноваться, ЛЮБЫЕ разновидности заданий (дейлики, майлстоуны) с ЛЮБЫМИ наградами. Сильно бы освежило игру адекватное поднятие по рангу за соло-скилл, а не попытки сыграться с бездарями в команде, что только и ждут паса во вражеской части поля только для того, чтобы потом обижаться за наказание такого поведения. И понятное дело, что футбол всегда был и будет про командное взаимодействие, но менталка некоторых игроков просто вымораживает, что в 5v5 доходит до абсурда. И это говорит тот, кто в любых компететив играх показывает себя ниже среднего. Понимаете, насколько тут все плохо с коммьюнити? 

Зато что уж очень любят разработчики так это коллабы и кучу косметики. Само собой, за деньги. Или в бпшнике, который качается ну просто безумно долго и туго. И само собой, тоже покупается за деньги. Действительно, зачем работать, если можно продать приколов?

Это все, впрочем, мысли ни о чем, потому что с таким подходом игра растеряет и так уже малый онлайн очень быстро, и останутся только энтузиасты, а поиск матча будет занимать больше, чем сам матч. Чтобы один игрок не загрузился.. и все по новой. 

Но справедливости ради, стоит отметить, что последний бета-тест порадовал. Разработчики представили режим 1 на 1. Вот на него действительно хочется посмотреть в релизной версии обновления. Наконец-то будет место, где можно будет повеселиться и отработать дрибблинг. Как выразился мой друг - "похоже на дворовой футбол". Есть в этом свой шарм. Но онлайн это вряд ли спасет. 

В конечном итоге, несмотря на дурку в студии у разработчиков, игру стоит хотя бы попробовать. Даже если вам, как и мне, до этого момента был безразличен футбол сам по себе. Опыт, по меньшей мере, интересный. И есть все шансы, что часов на 50 игра вас точно затянет. А если собрать друзей.. и если появится желание совершенствоваться и стать лучшим страйкером в традициях блю лока, то на все 200. Такое ни одна другая игра вам не предложит.

I wish there was a mixed option because I do/don't recommend this game at times. I enjoyed the launch and beginning state of Rematch but there was a lot of bugs and exploits to gain advantages, but as every game launch in recent years, we always have to expect bugs and glitches unfortunately.... 

The devs have patched out and put out balance changes to balance the game and I think, at least for me personally, its at a state where it seems the most balanced and focused on team play but for some reason its just not hitting like it did in the beginning days of Rematch. Not sure if I got bored or burnt out by the game but I play it every so often and still enjoy my time, unless just having bad games. 

I think what would be useful is different game modes, casual ones and ranked. The recent limited time gamemode was quite fun and more should be like that, but it did get stale after some time. Also maybe add some party style games? Just need something else to do. For ranked, 4v4s should be added since 3v3s was added. 

Also store updates need to have more items to change up the outfits and style, not just paid collabs. Someone could correct me if im wrong, but things in the battle pass wont appear in the store until the next season? If someone doesnt want the battlepass there should be more items available. Get that designer to cook up!

Overall, Its not a bad game, but its not a GREAT game yet..... as long as they continue to push out the correct updates. I would just say if you are getting frustrated at the game, just stop playing and play something else.

This game is fun to play with mates and all don't get me wrong however, there are so many things I can list and go on forever about these issues on how they affect the game and community. 

Cons:
- inconsistent player model hitbox registration. Sometimes the player doesn't gain control of the ball or the character isn't body blocking another player properly, sometimes they just ghost right through you. 
- delayed and inconsistent indicator timing. Whenever the ball is airborne and you're running to make a play or you're receiving a high ball from a goal kick or a long pass,, lots of players are already inputing their buttons to either receive the pass or shoot first time, the indicator suddenly disappears. The length between the player and the ball for the indicator to pop-up is misleading and often creates lots of counter-attacks because of it.
- hitbox registration with the ball. The hitbox registration with the ball itself, sometimes I would try to either pass, tackle or shoot the ball, my character would just go through, air- swing or is just unable to tap the ball at all.
- queueing into same players over several times within one sitting. When my mates and I try to re-queue right after a finished game, we wait out several minutes, just so we don't get match-made with the same opposing team because it's unfun or the ranking is just bizarre, making it harder to play.
- inconsistent ball control mechanics. I hear my friends raging a lot how their character doesn't keep the ball properly and ends up making the "wrong touch" or when you're trying to dribble, the inputs are delayed and inconsistent. 
- queue times in either game modes is unbelievably RNG, they are either so quick or they are so long. (you would most likely get matchmade into the same enemy team if you're 4/5 stack for 5v5s).
- defensive stance is also inconsistent as if they try to rotate their player (themselves), the running animation takes slowly gradualates and slows down immediately. 
- when you are trying to make a turn or do a 180 to challenge/chase/tackle or whatever, the character does the slow running animaiton on the run-up before running normally while it wastes like 5-10% stamina bar throughout this delayed action input. 
- deflections (blocking passes/shots etc.) is definitely one of the biggest downfalls of this game, when the ball deflects off a player, it deflect very unnanturally which leads to concedes so many goals and is frustrating to deal with. Sometimes when you are deflecting shots and passes, the ball either bounces too far or doesn't bounce at all (may end up being stationary). 

Pros:
- customisation is fun to use and makes the game enjoyable
- the pass indicator is smooth to notice and is very iconic
- textures are fine, have no problems with it to be honest
- mechanics can definitely be polished and reworked but they are definitely usable. 
- queue times are decently OK in 3v3s but 5v5s is very RNG depending. 

As much as I want to recommend this game to someone. I do not for the time being unless they make the F2P (which will attract more players and therefore you'll be getting more creative feedback and criticism on these upcoming updates and changes in regards to the game itself). 

It's not worth spending $40 on a game that barely keeps itself alive.

Sloclap is one of my favorite studios. I'll try anything they make because they're one of the few studios that still take risks with their games and ideas, and don't treat new players like babies. They're really not about making everyone a winner when you play their games. They don't care about making players feel good about losing just so they'll come back and play again, or softening the blow so to speak. 

When you get absolutely bodied by another player in Absolver, it's an embarrassment -- you've disgraced your whole family lineage, you leave the match with shame and regret. That's why most people didn't stream themselves playing it when it was at its peak player count (which is probably why the game didn't really take off like Sifu did). There aren't many games nowadays that can make an L feel so personal. There aren't many games that can bring back that feeling of watching yourself get teabagged in Halo 2.

Losing in a Sloclap game is a very humbling experience. When you finally make peace with defeat and start to realize, "Maybe I'm just not that guy," that's when the true game begins: you become just like Rocky or Daniel and train your ass off to get better, stronger, faster. Like a heartbroken man who goes on his gym bro arc, you start a journey of self-improvement so that you can never be hurt again. 

All of Sloclap's games are purely skill based and Rematch is no different. As many people found out with Sifu, you can't just pick up the controller and instantly be a chud. It actually took studying moves and mechanics, and actually practicing in the dojo in order to git gud. Every one of their titles has some sort of free play mode for this very reason, so you can hone your skills. 

Extreme difficulty curves in video games can be a turn off to most players and only appeals to a niche few, which is why I respect Sloclap for not making any compromises for the sake of gaining a wider audience. Rematch isn't nearly as difficult as Sifu but it still takes practice. It's actually kind of interesting that Sloclap choose to make a sports game because "practice makes perfect" is the philosophy of most athletes. 

Like a real soccer player, I didn't want to enter my first match with other players without practicing. I also didn't want to be the worse player on my team and cost us the game. So I spent my first couple of hours in the tutorial and free play mode until I felt ready enough to compete, and you can really tell which players did this and which didn't based on the way they played. 

Wins in this game feel important and satisfying because of what it takes to get one. It takes coordinated effort and knowledge of the controls. There's no cheesing your way to victory. You really have to earn it, especially if you're playing against a team that really knows the game. Again, highly competitive games like this aren't for everyone. After going on a losing streak, players will either strive to get better, or uninstall and refund, most choosing the latter. Most negative reviews are for this reason and because of some performance issues which I think will be worked out in due time. Sloclap has a reputation of fixing and improving their games overtime, even adding new content way past launch. 

With all that being said, I do have some complaints of my own and suggestions on how to improve/expand on the game:

1) If a player disconnects or rage quits during the middle of a match, they should be replaced with a bot  instantly until a new player joins the match. That would at least give the handicapped team a fighting chance.

2) There should be a camera option that follows the ball at all times (maybe a lock-on feature). The mini map helps with tracking the ball but not when it's way down field. And because of the fast-paced nature of the game, having to swing my camera around to follow the ball manually just doesn't cut it.

3) I don't know if this is actually doable yet but I'd love to see real sized soccer teams with real rules of the game: red cards, yellow cards, a ref. The fact there's no offsides, out of bounds, or penalty kicks is kind of lame. It's not even a soccer game at that point, it's Rocket League. Which is fine and all but I'd like more than just an arcade experience.

4) A way to designate team positions (i.e. some people shouldn't be goalie, some people are better at scoring, etc).

I think the foundation of Rematch is really strong and it's extremely creative, addictive and fun to play, and I will keep coming back to it. I would hate to see it suffer the same fate as Absolver, one of my favorite games ever made, I own it for console and PC. So please, Sloclap, keep on cooking! Either way, I'll still support your next project.

P.S. I think a Sifu 2 is in order.

The game is a lot of fun, but it has a LOT of problems. It feels the same as it did in the beta test.

Many connectivity issues when entering games.
- I join a match with two friends while in a party, and the game will start with me and one of the friends while the other never enters the game. 
- The ball randomly teleports to the goalie's hand, a player's foot, or just weirdly change course in midair.
- You may be kicked from a match if not enough players connect, but the game will not allow you to join another game because it still thinks you are in a match (many times we needed to restart the game to fix this).

Matchmaking also needs improvement, as you will find a lot of newbies despite the level of your party, even in ranked. And you may also find people with tens of hours after your first 3 games, like it happened to my party.

The goalkeeper still needs some adjustments too, as at times you really wished you had more options when it comes to defense, but other times a kick animation will be cancelled mid-air and the ball will halt at the goalie's hands.

There is a lack of options to make your online experience with random players (or even your own friends) more pleasant, like being able to mute people when they spam messages to pass for example. The game does put a limit, but it still allows people to spam the same thing about 5 times every few seconds, which makes focusing in the game very difficult. And it is absolutely infuriating to make a mistake and be told what a good job you did repeatedly for the rest of the match, even with muted microphones.

Cosmetics in the game are also very limited, which one would expect variety from considering this is live service. There is barely anything new since the game came out of beta, and the additions you can see are very... inconsequential.

The game is still very fun at it's core, but I wish the devs had done a better job at polishing it past the state I saw it in during the beta tests. It could have been great, but it is just fine. I would not recommend buying this at full price, wait until there are discounts, or until this eventually becomes free. Hope the game is improved in the future because with some more care it could become the game it deserved to be.

The downfall of this game has to be studied - as I'm typing this review right now, the number of players currently online is only sitting at a whopping 930 players.

Undeniably, there are issue that needs urgent fixing. I can never understand how with every update introduced, new exploits come along with it - people who constantly abuses "tech" which makes it hard to defend or attack. This makes the game very much unenjoyable.

Queuing up for games in South East Asia region has an average wait time of 12 minutes & that does not even guarantee you a game. Whereas playing in North East Asia servers only guarntees a higher blood pressure count due to how laggy it can get - there is a delay in every action executed whether it be tackle, shooting or passing.

I am not one to leave reviews for game but seeing the one game I enjoyed playing taking a nose dive doesn't sit right with me & I hope devs will take action. 

Currently, at the price point and with the constant hard selling of in-game items, game bugs & low player count, I would strongly not recommend it to anyone.

I have 277 hours, mostly spent playing quickplay, and the amount of egotistical Messi wannabes, paired with game breaking dribbling exploits simply make the game unfun. I will consider reinstalling once the dribbling exploits are fixed. (To be fair, the game is probably going to bleed out before that) Great job Sloclap, you managed to create another unique gaming experience, only to ruin it by crappy netcode, a premature release, and refusing to adress game-breaking bugs, just like Absolver!

Update: I played some of patch seven beta, and I have this to say.
It is my opinion that actively promoting and encouraging these "dribbling" mechanics only makes the wannabe messis happy, I have played 3v3 matches where there were maybe 2-3 shots on goal, but 5 minutes of a defender cautiously pressing such a "skilled" dribbler. Football is a team game, it is a passing sport. If we promote egoism, we only accelerate the pace at which players like me leave, and encourage a toxic "I'm gonna do it all" environment. That and it feels like goalie got kneecaped and can strafe at half the usual pace, which only means 1v1 against those types is a guaranteed loss.

This game is the new poster child for the industry at the moment. Releasing poorly tested garbage and not putting any care into the game they created. Every update makes this game worse, bringing new problems and changes that most players cant get used to. 

There are still game-breaking exploits "high level" players will use that make it impossible to stop a push towards the goal, and we are 6 months into this game's life. 

As someone who's been playing since the beta, I would NOT recommend buying the game at the moment. Sloclap has not done well in optimizing and fixing this game, and their intentions are obvious: Spend most of the funds into collaborations and designer brands, then give almost no funding towards the development team. This should still be in testing, as there are too many problems and inconsistencies that get in the way from actually enjoying the game.

Starting off, at the end of the day, this game attracted the wrong crowd of people. But let's be honest - the same could be said with any competitive game out there. Still though, I've experienced a very little amount of matches where the players aren't selfish, disgusting and just mentally horrible, especially due to this being related to football - which has a team based culture around it. 

These points apply for any rank in the game. Even elite players are still a bunch of arrogant, egoist dribblers instead of focusing on what should be the main focus - teamwork and making quick and effective counter plays. Due to these slow dribbler types, the complete point of a build up play goes out the window as your opponents rush and make their way back to their own box in no time, completely eliminating any passing options, besides back, which players never utilise for some odd reason.

Something goes wrong in a match though? They'd rather start throwing or AFKing, or throwing a tantrum like little fetuses, or when things go south, they expect you to wipe their ass clean of all the mistakes they made. The game is done for me. It's not attracting a fresh player base anymore and only decreasing, while keeping the same braindead players who think they are goated soloing at let me remind you - A TEAM BASED GAME, where the ball will always move faster than any players dribble.

Now to move on to the development side of things - very slow, and updates are usually bug ridden, making the overall gameplay experience worse, with patches taking a long time as well. What's worse is that one of my friends (this could or could not be true), said they recently as of the time of writing said they updated their TOS (Terms of Service) to mention that they can't guarantee servers are going to be consistently smooth anymore, which implies a lower budget and cheapening out on the, what in my opinion are, bad enough servers as they were before, with plenty of problems.

Finally, and this was completely optional as well, but they completely missed the opportunities with the cosmetics possible in this game. They limit themselves to real clothing brands and partnerships and sponsors instead of actually being really creative with what your character could wear, look like, and offer a crazy amount of customization options, possibly even incentivising players to hop into the store more often to support the game. I'm a big fan of cosmetic items in games usually, but in this game it's just really shallow.

As of time of writing, I simply cannot recommend this game to anyone sadly, as excited as I was for this game during the BETA and earlier on. They started off really on the strong foot in the first season, and have plateaued since. I will continue to play the game on occasion whenever I feel like playing it, but each time I do it's just not a pleasant experience, but I really do want this game to shine and change this review eventually.

As a football game, it has, [i]by nature[/i], one of the most toxic communities I’ve ever encountered. I've been screamed at, insulted, sometimes in languages I don't even understand, and even received a death threat. And still, it has proven to be one of the most fun multiplayer experiences I've enjoyed in close to a decade. 

Having played this game since day 1, it is clear to me that it was not quite ready for launch when it did launch. The growing pains it went through trying to balance the most egregious "meta" that was popular the following weeks left a lot of people disappointed and frustrated, both the users of said "meta" and its victims. By now, in my opinion, the game has arrived at a point where I'd call it to be a good position in terms of balance. Sure, it still feels unfair at times, but I've noticed that a lot of times it is not because teams were inherently unfairly balanced (though sometimes they are), but that my team just didn't [i]vibe[/i] as well as the opponent's did.
When this game works, [b]it works[/b]. It takes everything, that, for me, makes football boring to watch and incredibly boring to play and just [b]yeets[/b] that out the window. The last time I got this much enjoyment out of a football game was Fifa Street for the PS2 in 2006. Accuracy, reliability, positioning and teamwork don't rely on some random stats your team is made out of (or worse how much money you spent), but on the skill and attention of every single player. While this might lead to frustration sometimes, especially in 5v5, it is even more rewarding everytime your team succeeds. 
Being a live-service title I'm not surprised that there are micro transactions and a pass. Development is an ongoing cost and it needs an ongoing revenue stream. I am pleasantly surprised however that every item in the shop or the pass is cosmetic in nature and there being no space for pay 2 win. Its cosmetics (so far) are also not extremely ridiculous or completely unfitting to its visual style (though the halloween pack was cutting it very close in my opinion). 

Taking the well-established formula of football and being the first to try and turn it into an even more engaging and fun video game experience than any one has ever tried before is not an easy task and for the most part, the team at Sloclap has exceeded all my expectations. For its price of just 25€ I fully recommend Rematch. If you're unsure, the next Steam Sale is bound to happen or you might check out third-party sellers offering an even better deal.

하지만 먼저 실점이라도 하면 고의로 경기를 망치는 사람들이나 그에 대한 신고 체계가 존재하지 않았던 점이 가장 게임을 그만두게 만들었습니다. 뿐만 아니라 공이 고정되거나 사라지는 버그, 불안정한 네트워크, 전체적으로 아쉬운 완성도 등이 플레이 하면서 만족스럽지 못했습니다.

I got this game out of love for Sloclap. 

I never played FIFA, only watched ppl playing it, and thought that it would be something similar.

But if anything, it's more similar to Rocket League than anything else.

Tbh it's a very innovative concept that probably no game company has ever done or will do, since I honestly don't see the appeal of doing something you could just go out and do online. 

Unless you're injured/physically disabled or just moved away from a city where you had your best buddies in the local football club, and want to reconnect with them doing the thing you love the most, even if it's only online with some digital figurines. 

I said "just moved" because I would assume that after a while you also would've made friends at the new club.

Either way I'd say it's a very niche audience. If so, I'm glad that Sloclap created something for the first group I mentioned.

Sloclap please make a new fighting game. Absolver was the first baby step which put your name out, in Sifu you nailed the storytelling and a fluid control system, in Rematch you excelled at PvP (definitely better crafted than in Absolver), please use all you learned in the process to make a new multiplayer fighting game. PvE as a gang of friends against foes would also be cool. 

I could just go out and play football if I want to, but I can't/don't want to break my sparring partner's bones, and don't want to have my own bones broken either, make something that substitutes that, not football.

Recommended as encouragement.

【切り抜き】『REMATCH』は「サッカーゲーム」じゃなくて「責任押し付け合いゲーム」です。

えー、スパチャありがとうございます。「Sloclapの新作『REMATCH』の3vs3やってますか？ FIFAと違って味方ガチャが酷い気がします」と。

んーと、これ結論から言うと、「味方ガチャ」とか言ってる時点で、あなたの実力が足りてない証拠ですよ。

あの、FIFAとかウイイレって、結局「監督」の視点なんですよね。 パス出した先でAIが動いてくれたり、守備をオートでやってくれたりするじゃないですか。だから負けた時に「AIがバカだった」って言い訳ができるんですよ。

でも『REMATCH』って、完全にキャラ単体を操作するTPS（三人称視点）のアクションゲームなんですよね。 パスがズレたのも、シュート外したのも、裏を取られて失点したのも、全部「操作してる人間（あなた）のミス」なんですよ。

これ、「言い訳の余地がない」っていう意味で、めちゃくちゃ残酷なゲームなんです。

特に3vs3って、一人あたりの責任が重すぎるんですよ。 5vs5なら一人サボってもバレないかもしれないですけど、3人で一人穴がいたら、もうその時点で数的不利でボコボコにされるじゃないですか。

で、「味方がパスくれない」って文句言う人いますけど、それ「パスを出したくなるような動きをあなたがしてないから」ですよね？ TPS視点だから、味方の視界に入ってないだけかもしれないし、単純にあなたが信用されてないだけなんですよ。

このゲーム、流行りの『ブルーロック』みたいなもんで、全員が「俺がゴール決めたい！」ってエゴの塊で動いてるんですよ。 その中でボールを回してもらうには、実力を見せつけるしかないんです。

だから、「野良の味方がゴミだ」って嘆く暇があったら、自分がドリブルで3人抜いてゴール決めればいい話じゃないですか？ それができないなら、文句言う資格ないと思いますよ。

結局、このゲームを楽しめるのって、「俺のミスは俺のせい、俺のゴールは俺のおかげ」っていう、個人主義の極みみたいな思考ができる人だけなんですよね。

「みんなで仲良くパス回して…」みたいな体育の授業みたいなサッカーがしたいなら、悪いこと言わないんでFIFAのプロクラブでもやってた方が幸せになれますよ。

『REMATCH』は、性格悪いやつか、圧倒的に上手いナルシストしか生き残れない修羅の国なんで。 メンタル弱い人は回れ右したほうがいいと思います。はい。

Loved the look of this game and at the start it felt amazing. Getting good with the controls felt worthwhile and satisfying. But then the bugs came. I was constantly stuck in corners and losing the ability to interact with the ball. So i left it for a bit but its been weeks and now the only thing thats consistent is the inconsistency of in-game interactions. As the game state is right now, i cannot recommend it. I say this entirely as someone who would love to see this game succeed as it felt amazing before the cracks started showing. Edit: came back several months later and STILL getting bugs with the ball going through people and goals happening after the keeper has already saved and kicked the ball away. DO NOT PLAY THIS GAME. given how long ive left it, its clear the devs have no intention of fixing this game.

REMATCH is honestly a really fun and surprisingly addictive game. Playing it with friends is an absolute blast, and the core gameplay feels great.

The only downside is that the player base keeps shrinking. Every month there seem to be fewer people online, which makes matchmaking harder than it should be. I really hope the devs try to promote the game more or maybe even lower the price a bit to bring in new players — it definitely deserves more attention.
There’s also the ping issue. In Azerbaijan the ping is usually around 90–95, which isn’t very comfortable to play with. It would be awesome if this could be improved somehow.
Despite these problems, the game itself is fantastic. I’d love to see it get the support it needs to stay alive.

A nice mix up for sports games since I have never been a real fan of playing them typically. I was always of the opinion that I would rather play them in real life than on the screen however this game changed that opinion and showed me that it was the presentation and involvemnet that I felt was lacking.

While the game has its fair share of problems, bugs, and a cash shop while limiting other aspects of character creation, it was a pleasure to play and the gameplay was a fresh take on the sports genre as a whole. I am interested to see where they go or what other devs take from this to potentially make more action oriented sports games.

If you want to play the game with friends, it's really a lot of fun.

But if you want to play with randoms, you really have to be able to control yourself. Most randoms react far too late, don't pass the ball, and try to score goals themselves without the team, so-called egoists. It also often happens that some randoms have no logical thinking at all when it comes to game decisions. There are also people who constantly shout “pass!” and “keeper!”, which is really annoying.

But let's move on to one of the updates to the game. The new Fireball mode blocks Quickmatch 4vs4/5vs5. This forces players to either play Quickmatch 3vs3, play ranked, because 5vs5 works there, or play the new mode. 
I'm sorry, but that's one of the dumbest decisions ever. I really hope that no other modes will be blocked in the next new game mode.

**EDIT January 25th**
It's gotten really bad when playing 5v5 ranked games. A lot of people leave a game without rejoining. So you have to play with fewer players for the rest of the game, which can make a big difference.

**Edit May 18th**

A direção que o jogo tomou recentemente deixou-me completamente perdido. As decisões do estúdio parecem cada vez mais desconectadas com o que a comunidade realmente quer. Em vez de melhorar a experiência, focaram-se em conteúdos superficiais e mudanças que só tornam tudo mais frustrante. Entre bugs constantes, desempenho instável e escolhas criativas difíceis de compreender, aquilo que me fez apaixonar pelo jogo foi-se apagando aos poucos.

É triste ver algo com tanto potencial ser desperdiçado desta maneira.

Nie jestem najlepszy w pisanie recenzji więc moje zdanie pewnie nie zmieni waszej decyzji co do zakupu tej gry. Choć uważam ją za świetną i na pewno wartą swojej ceny która jest co najmniej 3 razy tańsza w swojej standardowej cenie od Fc 26, randomy potrafią zepsuć zabawę z rozgrywki (szczególnie gdy nie podają i tracą piłkę za każdym). Na szczęście częściej się trafia na graczy wiedzących co robią. Rywali dobiera mi w max minutę. Chcesz grać piłką? Masz tryb pięcio osobowy. Chcesz kiwać się i strzelać mnóstwo bramek? Masz tryb 3vs3. Strzelanie  i podawanie zrobione solidnie. Gra ogólnie jest o wiele lepsza niż fc (przynajmniej dla mnie)

This game has its moments of fun, however the dev team seems to be doing everything possible to kill this game.. Took way too long to add any content including the seasons (battle pass) essentially, 90% of the stuff that was added is locked behind paywalls. On top of that they force players to play specific "limited time game modes" and lock the traditional game mode for a week while the event is going on. Connections issues left and right on average 7-10 min wait times between matches often ending up in the same game as the people you've just played with. This game is still enjoyable for whatever reason but the game is destined to fail and the price point does not accurately represent the amount of content or complexity of the game as a whole. Overall you're better off saving your money for something with active devs and updates

!!!!!!!DONT BUY THIS GAME AT ALL!!!!!!!

After 178 hours of this game and really finding my way into it and playing it i dont recomend to play this game at all.

Postive is that the outfits are very nice same as the celebration poses and how you can make the stadium look

NEGATIVE IS A LOT!!

- Manipulators

You have a lot of ppl who sabotage the game on purpose and this doesnt happen rarely it happens a lot and this is extremly frustrating especially if you do ranked games. You dont know if the reporting works properly so you cant know if they are banned or whatever. They shoot in your own goal - they keep the goal occupied and dont try to hold the ball or they stand in a corner.

Ranked Games

Existing Ranks from low to high

Bronze 1 Bronze 2 Bronze 3             Silver same 1 2 3         Gold 1 2 3    Platinum 1 2 3 (not sure if there are more) 

BUT that doesnt matter because the ranked game system is like lets say if we go 5vs5. 2 of those players can be gold 1 of the player can be bronze 1, 1 can be silver 1 and one can be platinum.

As you see it really doesnt matter - the enemy team could have 3 gold players and 2 silver players - you basicailly need pure luck to have higher ranked ppl that should have some knowledge how to play instead of newbies that dont know how to pass at all or take the ball etc.


Glitches and Bugs

Disconnects: Many times it happens ppl discconect from the game like literally many times from 10 times i would say it happens atleast 3-4 times and if it happens in your team it makes it obviously so much harder to maybe win

Puppet Mode: If you disconnect and rejoin the game (even after restart the whole game)  there is a quite high possiblity that you wont be able to do anything besides sprint. you wont be able to take the ball, you cant tackle,  you cant block ppl to slow them down to help somehow, you cant hold the ball when you goal keeper nothing. Just being a puppet and stay in the corner because if you leave in rank you lost the game and lost points.

Currently when i write this review there are some more lags during the game as the servers are not stable right now where everyone shifts around and is suddenly at postion x - idk when this will be fixed tho
Unfortunately, it's a disappointment.
